title: Multistage telescopic jack lift assembly
abstract: The present disclosure pertains to a multistage telescopic jack lift assembly which comprises a plurality of internally and externally threaded pipes arranged in telescopic manner engaging with each other through their corresponding internal and external threads. The plurality of pipes are arranged in an internally threaded base pipe that engages with outer most of the plurality of pipes through its internal thread engaging with external thread of the outer most of the plurality of pipes. A platform fixed to the innermost of the pipes is raised up or lowered when the base pipe is rotated in one or other direction by means of a worm and gear arrangement run by a motor. The platform is prevented from rotation by means of a set of telescopic column that allow the platform to be raised or lowered but prevent its rotation. The arrangement is mounted on a transportable base.
claims: Claims I Claim: 1. A multistage telescopic jack lift assemblycomprising: a plurality of pipes, wherein the plurality of pipes are internally and externally threaded and arranged in telescopic manner engaging with each other through their corresponding internal and external threads; an internally threaded base pipe configured to house the plurality of pipes and engaging with outer most of the plurality of pipes through its internal thread engaging with external thread of the outer most of the plurality of pipes; a platform fixed to the innermost of the plurality of pipes; and a means to rotate the base pipe; wherein rotation of the base pipe in one or other direction results in raising or lowering of the platform. 2. The lift assembly of claim 1, wherein the lift assembly further incorporates means to prevent rotation of the platform. 3. The lift assembly of claim 2, wherein the means to prevent rotation of the platform are a set of telescopic columns that allow the platform to be raised or lowered but prevent its rotation. 4. The lift assembly of claim 1, wherein the means to rotate the base pipe is a set of worm and gear, the gear fixed to the base pipe and the worm driven by a motor. 5. The lift assembly of claim 1, wherein the plurality of pipes and the base pipe incorporate locking mechanism configuredto ensure that the plurality of pipes have adequate engagement with each other in extended condition. 6. The lift assembly of claim 5, wherein the locking mechanism is end of internal thread with relief at appropriate position. 7. The lift assembly of claim 1, wherein the lift assembly further incorporates a base support configured to enable transporting the lift assembly from one place to other. 8. The lift assembly of claim 7, wherein the base support configured to provide a firm support to the lift assembly at work place, wherein the firm support to the lift assembly is provided by means of plurality of screw jack arrangements and the plurality of screw jack arrangements help in positioning the lift assembly on uneven ground. 9. The lift assembly of claim 8, wherein the plurality of screw jack arrangements are mounted on plurality of threaded adjustable shafts, wherein the threaded adjustable shafts are configured to extent the plurality of screw jack arrangements and reduce or increase size of the base support depending on available space 10. The lift assembly of claim 8, wherein the base pipe is fixed on the base support by means of bearings configured to enable rotation of the base pipe and provide firm support to the base pipe.
